content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Ran `git diff` (worktree clean aside from untracked `.ralph-burning/`), reviewed the effective checkpoint delta in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs` and `src/adapters/fs.rs` end-to-end, and ran `cargo test milestone_record::service --lib`. I found three correctness issues in lifecycle reopen/recovery handling.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1500-1527` now reopens `completed`/`failed` milestones by directly mutating `snapshot.status = MilestoneStatus::Planning`, and `materialize_bundle` then emits only a `planning -> ready` transition (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1292-1301`). The snapshot write at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1537-1546` persists that terminal-state change without any `StatusChanged` event from the old terminal state, so the journal can jump from `running -> completed` straight to `planning -> ready`. That bypasses `allows_transition_to`, violates the “invalid transitions are rejected” contract, and leaves lifecycle replay with an impossible transition sequence.
   Rationale: This patch introduces a real state-machine correctness bug: terminal milestones are now reopened through an unjournaled, otherwise-invalid transition.
2. `build_reconciled_transition_events` stamps every synthesized bridge event with the single `now` argument (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:718-773`). On the missed-start recovery path, `update_task_run` passes the completion timestamp as that `now` even though `finalized_run.started_at` is available (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1856-1890`). That means a repaired `ready -> running` event is recorded at finish time instead of actual start time. The immediate `completed`/`failed` metadata then falls back to `previous_snapshot.updated_at` in `accumulated_running_duration_seconds` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:540-571`), which can overstate runtime, and later duration calculations see a zero-length historical run.
   Rationale: The recovery path now produces incorrect lifecycle timestamps and duration metadata on a supported failure-repair flow.
3. `accumulated_running_duration_seconds` sums every prior running segment in the whole journal without checking which plan version the events belong to (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:546-571`), even though lifecycle events already record `plan_version` in metadata (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:617-619`). After `persist_plan_locked` increments `plan_version` and reopens a terminal milestone for a materially changed bundle (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1500-1527`), the next `completed`/`failed` transition will include runtime from the previous plan incarnation as well. A reopened milestone that finishes a second time will therefore report an inflated `duration_seconds`.
   Rationale: Once rematerialization can reopen completed milestones, the current duration accumulator becomes incorrect across plan versions.
